messages.text: What custom tool would you like to build for this space? (e.g., Kanban board, project dashboard, asset tracker)
build a kanban board to track our work

**Considering Project Context**

Okay, I'm now focusing on the available workspace files. I've begun to digest the "UX Research plan" and the overview presentation for Project Ollie. These documents provide crucial background, especially the baseline workflow and agent delegation, which will likely inform how we structure the kanban board. I'm looking for key tasks and stages.


**Analyzing Project Data**

I've shifted focus to new documents, including the "Research Sprint Readout" and notes from daily syncs. I'm noting the project's name, "Project Ollie," and key individuals like Cowboy Lynk and Mike Carcasole. The sync notes reveal various tracks: Teamfood, with sub-tracks such as Coding Agent and Agent Quality, that appear to have some deadlines or expectations. It’s useful to see these team structures.


**Prioritizing Task Structure**

I'm now zeroing in on specific task areas within Project Ollie, particularly production, design/UXR, and product aspects. I'm noting key individuals and deadlines, especially the in-person codesign session with Workspace customers next week. I've also identified focus areas like data privacy and UX/UI refinements from recent communications. Crucially, I'm thinking about how the Kanban board will support these activities, emphasizing synchronization. I recall the instruction to create self-contained HTML files.


**Generating Code Directly**

I've got it! No tools, just code. The goal is an immediately usable `index.html` application. Forget PRDs and design docs – straight to the output. I also have to remember the exact sync API instructions.


**Implementing Sync Functions**

Okay, I'm now structuring the sync functions for the application, specifically the `loadSyncedState` and `saveSyncedState` functions. My plan is to utilize fetch to interact with the `/api/sync` endpoints, with careful error handling. I'll focus on storing and retrieving Kanban board state. The critical thing here is to make the API calls efficient and robust. The `syncKey` will be `"board_state_data"`.


**Identifying Action Items**

I'm now focusing on actionable items from recent updates. I see several "to-do" tasks including addressing scheduled jobs and chat streaming bugs, along with a bug bash and quality list. There's also UX/UI refinements and a data privacy audit to complete. Additionally, I'm noting the in-person codesign sessions next week. Finally, I'm taking note of the prototype testing process and the completed navigation build.


**Designing Kanban Aesthetics**

I'm now focused on the UI. The goal is to create a dynamic, intuitive Kanban board within the existing framework. I envision a clean dashboard with interactive columns: "To Do," "In Progress," "In Review," and "Done." Real-time multiplayer synchronization using `/api/sync/:envId/ollie_kanban_board` is the cornerstone. I'm adding card creation (title, description, assignee, priority, track, due date) and implementing robust movement options: drag-and-drop or simple click-to-move buttons. I also plan to add track filters to easily manage the growing list.


**Refining Board Visuals**

I'm now implementing the UI design elements. I'm focusing on a modern, minimalist aesthetic. I'm adding deep slate accents and leveraging Tailwind CSS, with Lucide icons (loaded via CDN or SVG) for a clean look, including good typography and styling for tags. I'm also planning detailed console logging for easy debugging, and thinking about collaborative cursor display, or at least showing workspace details. Filter/search capabilities and task statistics are also on my roadmap. I will use the pre-populated task details to get started. I will also incorporate modern HTML5 features to make the application even better.



**Prioritizing Tasks Effectively**

I'm now integrating new task items into the system. I'm focusing on "Scheduled Jobs & Chat Streaming Bugs," prioritizing these alongside the bug bash and quality list. I'll need to figure out who's assigned what, their track, and make it fit the overall structure. My main objective is to keep things synchronized across various areas. I'm taking the tasks and prioritizing them, which are due soon.


**Updating Task Integration**

I'm now integrating the new tasks, focusing on the Security & Data Privacy Audit and the In-Person Workspace Co-Design Sessions. I'm noting key individuals and deadlines, particularly the July 15th audit deadline. I need to fit these tasks into the current structure. I'm currently prioritizing these items alongside other tasks. The goal is to ensure a smooth workflow.


**Incorporating Sample Tasks**

I'm now integrating pre-populated sample tasks into the Kanban board for a more robust start. I'm focusing on "dscout screener," "Autopush Build Deployment," and "Giuditta/van Esch." I see the value in these examples for workflow, UI, and integration. I will utilize these samples for testing and development, which includes setting up my own due dates. I'm prioritizing tasks to ensure the new data fits the overall structure.


**Designing Board Interface**

I'm now focusing on designing UI/UX elements for the Ollie Workspace Kanban board. I'm prioritizing the header, including the title "Ollie Workspace Kanban," the environment name display (`window.__sandboxWorkspaceName`), the sync status badge ("Synced"), quick filter options, a search bar for tasks, and a "+ New Task" button. Next, I am thinking about the visual design of the cards. I'm focusing on modern minimalism.


**Implementing Drag-and-Drop**

I'm now focusing on drag-and-drop functionality for the Kanban board cards. I'm implementing standard HTML5 drag-and-drop mechanics with `draggable="true"`, using `ondragstart` to set the card ID in `dataTransfer`, `ondragover` to allow drops, and `ondrop` to update card status, save state, and re-render. I'm aiming for a smooth, intuitive user experience.


**Refining Synchronization Strategy**

I'm now refining the sync strategy, prioritizing fast response times. I'll maintain local state with `localStorage` fallback and fully sync using `fetch` with `/api/sync/:envId/:key` for robustness. I'm focusing on user experience, implementing automatic avatar initials and pastel colors for assignees like "Mike Carcasole," with the goal of creating a professional and elegant aesthetic. My goal is a fast and responsive application.


**Constructing Core Application**

I'm now building out the core Kanban board structure with HTML and Tailwind CSS. I'm focusing on the main layout with columns for "To Do," "In Progress," "In Review," and "Done." I'm implementing card placeholders with basic styling. I'll include placeholder tasks in each category. The goal is a clear, responsive, and functional skeleton.


**Developing Feature Implementation**

I'm now integrating crucial features into the Kanban board. I'm focusing on task editing functionality, task search capabilities, and the track filtering system. I'm adding a visual statistics dashboard that will be collapsible to save space. I'll add real-time multiplayer synchronization with short-polling every 2 seconds. The code must implement standard input focus preservation to ensure that text fields do not lose focus on state updates. I also plan to only load new states if there is a change.


**Implementing Sync Safeguards**

I'm now implementing safeguards to ensure smooth data synchronization. I'm focusing on preventing overwrites during active card editing using `isEditing` or modal status. I'll compare `JSON.stringify` of synced and local task states, updating only if there's a change and no active editing. I am planning on making this highly robust!

I'm also designing a dashboard for statistics. I'll display the total task count, tasks in progress, percentage completed, and a track representation with colored dots. I'm also planning the design for the tracks with colors defined like: `Teamfood`: Pink/Indigo, `Design/UXR`: Amber/Orange, `Production`: Teal/Emerald, `Product/Design`, with backgrounds and text colors. I'm looking forward to getting the dashboard and track colors done.


Here is a fully functional, collaborative Kanban board tailored specifically for **Project Ollie**. 

This application comes pre-populated with actual tasks and workstreams compiled directly from the **Ollie daily sync notes**, **UX Research plans**, and team timelines. It features full **real-time multiplayer synchronization** (the Synchronizer API) to keep your workspace collaborative and aligned.

### Features
* **Zero-Setup Real-Time Synchronization**: Seamless multiplayer status synchronization via short-polling to `/api/sync/` so your team sees updates live.
* **Ollie Team Context Pre-Loaded**: Pre-populated with real tracks (Teamfood, Design/UXR, Production, Product/Design) and active tasks (Mike Carcasole's Coding Agent, Cowboy Lynk's streaming bugs, Gabor's privacy audit, Daniel & Mirjam's NYC co-design sessions).
* **Robust Interaction**: Drag-and-drop support, quick-move control arrows, filters, and full task creation/editing.
* **Clean, Minimalist Aesthetics**: An elegant, modern look built with Tailwind CSS, Plus Jakarta Sans typography, and clear status indicators.

This application comes pre-populated with actual tasks and workstreams compiled directly from the **Ollie daily sync notes**, **UX Research plans**, and team timelines. It features full **real-time multiplayer synchronization** (the Synchronizer API) to keep your workspace collaborative and aligned. ### Features * **Zero-Setup Real-Time Synchronization**: Seamless multiplayer status synchronization via short-polling to `/api/sync/` so your team sees updates live. * **Ollie Team Context Pre-Loaded**: Pre-populated with real tracks (Teamfood, Design/UXR, Production, Product/Design) and active tasks (Mike Carcasole's Coding Agent, Cowboy Lynk's streaming bugs, Gabor's privacy audit, Daniel & Mirjam's NYC co-design sessions). * **Robust Interaction**: Drag-and-drop support, quick-move control arrows, filters, and full task creation/editing. * **Clean, Minimalist Aesthetics**: An elegant, modern look built with Tailwind CSS, Plus Jakarta Sans typography, and clear status indicators. ```html Project Ollie - Workspace Kanban
Project Ollie Local Mode

Workspace Kanban Board

Real-time collaborative task tracker for teamfood, UXR, and workspace integrations.

Active: 0
Completed: 0
Team Speed: Autopush v0

To Do

0

In Progress

0

In Review

0

Done

0

Create Task

```

sandboxFiles.name: [UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation
sandboxFiles.content:
Core Objective
To map the data trails, tool ecosystems, and context-sharing habits of knowledge workers in the real world, as well as their desires and expectations regarding AI delegation.
Participants
* Location & Availability: Must currently live in the U.S. and be able to attend a 90-minute, in-person study session at the NYC Google Office between July 6th and July 24th with their own laptop.1
* Company Profile: Employed at a private educational institute or a commercial business/organization (excluding public education, nonprofits, and government).1
* Software & Tool Usage:
   * Their company must use Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, or Calendar) with official work email addresses.1
   * The "actual work" they do must involve co-editing shared documents and spreadsheets (such as Google Docs or Sheets). Users who primarily manage tasks in dedicated project management tools (like Jira, Asana, or Trello) are disqualified.1
* Role & Authority: Must have a role in their business's decision to purchase digital tools and software (either as a sole decision-maker, part of a collective group, or as an influencer).1
* Collaboration Level: They must spend at least half or more of their work week collaborating with others on projects, tasks, or shared goals.1
Details here: Ollie current needs and painpoints 


Research Questions 
* RQ1 (The Fragmented Truth): What does a knowledge worker's true "to-do list" actually look like across formal tools (Jira, Asana) and informal channels (Slack stars, open browser tabs, mental notes)?
* RQ2 (Context Breadcrumbs): When a user moves a task forward, what specific artifacts (links, documents, specific chat sentences) represent the "proof of work" that Ollie will eventually need to ingest?
* RQ3 (The Translation Gap): How do users naturally summarize a complex day of work into a project update? What details do they filter out, and what do they consider critical?
* RQ4 (Agent Delegation Triggers): What tasks do users actually want to hand off versus what they want to keep control over? What parameters (triggers, boundaries, outputs) define a successful Sojo Agent assignment?
Session script
Artifact & Task Audit (15 mins)
* Goal: Uncover the hidden, informal task ecosystems.
* Activity: Have them open their laptop and show you their current "active stack."
* Prompts to use:
   * "Show me the 3 things you know you have to do today but aren't written down in a formal project board."
   * "Look at your open browser tabs or flagged emails right now—what tasks do those represent?"
   * "Where is the conversation happening about your top priority right now? Can you show me that Slack thread/email chain?"
The "Human Ollie" Walkthrough (25 mins)
* Goal: Treat the user's memory and screen as the database to see what Ollie should have done.
* Activity: Walk backward through their last 24–48 hours of work. Have them literally point at things on their screen.
* Prompts to use:
   * "You opened this Google Doc at 10 AM and left a comment. If Ollie was a smart assistant watching this, what is the 'core project update' it should extract from that action?"
   * "If an AI wrote an update based purely on this Slack thread you just showed me, what would it miss? What's the unwritten context here?"
   * "Look at this completed task. What were all the scattered pieces of data (spreadsheets, links, client feedback) that were required to get it done?"
AI Agents tasks (15 mins)
* Goal: Use their real data to define the scope and boundaries of the agents.
* Activity: Review the real tasks identified in Phase 1 and 2. Print out blank "Sojo Agent Job Description" cards (or use a digital whiteboard) and fill them out together.
* Prompts to use:
   * "Look at this specific task you spent an hour on yesterday. If an AI Agent could have done it, what is the exact trigger to start it? (e.g., 'When a new CSV lands in this folder' or 'When a client pings me via email')."
   * "What does the agent need access to on your laptop to successfully do this?"
   * "What is the exact output you expect? A draft email? A spreadsheet update? And where should it put it?"

Updates
* Eng

* Product

      * (1) handle the firehose (reactive)
      * (2) make progress towards goals (proactive)

            * Collaborative artifacts, etc

   * Drive Projects opportunity
* Design
   * Homepage

* Aiming to have an updated version of the prototype so Zhengrong can share with CT June 22
* UXR: 
   * Unmoderated survey and video responses (~90 ppl) https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/10PLeRSRBFKLA26X5RSrF6CsGG8mEJIrD9OwaUu3bYCA/edit?slide=id.g3eaea0c1acc_0_7498#slide=id.g3eaea0c1acc_0_7498
      * Projects frequently originate in live meetings or email/chat communications.
      * Workers prefer asynchronous methods like chat or email to clarify vague tasks.
      * Shared documents are the primary environment for actual collaborative work.
      * Task tracking is often manual, fragmented across tools, or reliant on memory
      * Progress updates are typically manual and ad-hoc, often depending on informal syncs
      * Teams predominantly perform manual verification of AI-generated content instead of using automation.
   * Come up with agenda/plan for UXR by June 15
      * Which type of profile are we looking to recruit
         * PGM (Charles?)
         * Operations (teams that work on short term project for other teams, like UXI, HR, Rews)
         * And i guess really anyone we can get


   * Next hosting interviews tomorrow and monday


:
________________


Jun 9, 2026 | Project Friday daily sync
Attendees: Cowboy Lynk Sascha Negele Mike Carcasole Zhengrong Ji Ian Mendiola Simon Sok Belinda Preno Michelle Haq Mirjam van Esch Gabor Cselle


Discussion
* Your inferred projects and tasks; configuration and reliability
* Single vs multiplayer
* Current friction points:
   * Keeping tasks up to date is work, though the agent makes it a bit easier
   * I can only delegate a subset of tasks to an agent




Updates
* Eng
* Product
* Design
   * Homepage Update (Suggestions, feed)
   * Zero state (start with stream)
* UXR (cannot join in person because of summit, uxr updates and asks below)


This week survey and interviews:
We have acquired the budget for the following screening, survey, and interview plans.
We are kicking of a DScout (UXR platform) project.
* We aim to send out screener today. The screener identifies eligible participants (employed and collaborate with others as part of their role. We will select participants across segment, roles and screen for good quality respondents)
Ask: review screener.. Sending out screener right after this meeting. 
* Next, we will collect 100 survey responses from participants selected in the screening—comprising multiple-choice, open-ended, and video inputs—regarding current workflows for initiating and scoping collaboration projects, tracking tasks, updating collaborators, and using AI in team-based tasks.
Ask: review the survey
Sending out survey (to any screened on participants) as soon as possible. 
* Finally, we will invite 20-50 of the survey respondents for a deep-dive 30-minute interview, tentatively scheduled for Friday, June 12th, and Monday, June 15th. but we should monitor feasibility based on availability and survey fill rate. Ask should we reserve some of the interviews to host post summit concept feedback testing 
                
Next week Recruiting onsite participants: Since the timeline is too tight to bring in external customers, we should focus on recruiting Googlers instead.
